notes.review: Thorough code reviewer; effective at catching logic issues.
notes.spec: Latest GPT-5 generation; strong at structured spec drafting.

notes.spec_review: Reliable spec reviewer; covers most issues in typical specs.
notes.implement: Good implementation quality; well-suited for mid-complexity features.
notes.review: Good code review; catches common patterns and obvious issues.
notes.spec: Reliable spec drafter; good balance of reasoning and speed.
notes.research: Strong research synthesis at GPT-5 tier; good at structured multi-source analysis.

notes.spec_review: Quick spec scan; may miss nuanced issues in complex specs.
notes.implement: Good for simple to mid-complexity features at lower cost.
notes.review: Fast review of straightforward changes; less effective on complex code.
notes.spec: Cost-effective for simple spec drafts; weaker on complex requirements.
notes.research: Limited depth for open-ended research synthesis; better for targeted lookups.

notes.spec_review: Good for technical spec review; strong code reasoning.
notes.implement: Code-optimized; particularly strong at implementation tasks.
notes.review: Strong code review; focused on technical correctness.
notes.spec: Codex-optimized variant; strong at code-adjacent spec writing.
notes.research: Code-optimised; poor at open-ended research synthesis and judgment tasks.

notes.spec_review: Reliable spec review for standard complexity.
notes.implement: Solid implementation quality for most features.
notes.spec: Earlier GPT-5 generation; stable and predictable.
notes.research: Solid research capability; earlier generation but well-understood behavior.

placeholders.AGENT_DEV_SERVER_NOTE:
> **CRITICAL — Codex dev server rules:**
>
> **NEVER run `npm run dev`, `next dev`, or any dev command directly.** Running dev commands directly bypasses port allocation and will cause port conflicts.
>
> The PORT is already set in `.env.local` by the worktree setup. If you must start a dev server, use `aigon dev-server start`.
>
> Because Codex kills background processes when shell commands exit, use `--register-only` mode:
>
> 1. Run `aigon dev-server start --register-only` — allocates your agent's port and writes it to `.env.local`
> 2. Read the allocated port: `grep ^PORT= .env.local`
> 3. Start the server in a **persistent terminal session** using `/terminal`: `PORT=<the-port> npm run dev`
> 4. Get your app URL: `aigon dev-server url`
> 5. Use that URL for all testing
